text: A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 3 cm. What is the perimeter of the rectangle?
generatorSolution:
To find the perimeter of a rectangle, add up all four sides.

Perimeter = length + width + length + width
Perimeter = 8 cm + 3 cm + 8 cm + 3 cm = 22 cm

Alternatively, use the formula: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width) = 2 × (8 + 3) = 2 × 11 = 22 cm

The answer is 22 cm.
